'''Chris Hicks''' (born 19 March 1977) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s, 2000s and 2010s. He last played for the [[Parramatta Eels]] of the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL).<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> A former New South Wales country origin representative {{rlp|fb}} or {{rlp|wg}}, he has previously played in the NRL for the [[Penrith Panthers]] and [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] as well as playing for [[Warrington Wolves]] of the [[Super League]].

==Playing career==
[[File:Chris Hicks.jpg|thumb|left|Hicks playing for Warrington in 2008]]

===Early NRL career===
Hicks made his NRL début for the Penrith Panthers in 1997. In six years with the Penrith club, Hicks scored just under 300 points.  In 2004, Hicks made his return to the NRL, this time for the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]].  Hicks spent four years with the Manly outfit and played in their [[National Rugby League season 2007#Grand final|2007 NRL grand final]] defeat to the [[Melbourne Storm]].<ref>{{cite news| url=http://www.news.com.au/dailytelegraph/story/0,22049,24415751-5001023,00.html| work=The Daily Telegraph| title=Factfile on 2007 grand final| date=29 September 2008| access-date=24 November 2008| archive-date=22 October 2008| archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081022115323/http://www.news.com.au/dailytelegraph/story/0,22049,24415751-5001023,00.html| url-status=dead}}</ref>

===Super League===
Hicks signed for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]] for the 2008 season.<ref name="Kite and Watmough re-signed with the Sea Eagles, Hicks to the Wolves">{{cite web|url=http://www.manlyseaeagles.com.au/news.asp?newsid=2022|title=Kite and Watmough re-signed with the Sea Eagles, Hicks to the Wolves|publisher=Manly Sea Eagles|date=21 June 2007|accessdate=21 June 2007 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070906031928/http://www.manlyseaeagles.com.au/news.asp?newsid=2022 |archive-date=6 September 2007}}</ref> At 30 years old he joined [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly]] team-mate [[Michael Monaghan]] in moving to the [[Warrington Wolves|Wolves]] for the 2008 season.<ref name=" Warrington sign Manly wing Hicks">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/warrington/6225294.stm|title= Warrington sign Manly wing Hicks|publisher=BBC|date=21 June 2007|access-date=21 June 2007}}</ref>

In his first season with Warrington, he scored in every single game after adopting kicking duties for the club. The first time he failed to score in a Warrington game came in a 60–8 defeat away at Harlequins on Saturday 24 March 2009, five games into his second season with the Warrington side.  In each of his three years at Warrington, Hicks was the team's top try-scorer. While he was with Warrington, the team won two Challenge Cups. Hicks scored a [[hat-trick]] in the [[2010 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Leeds 6-30 Warrington">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8938067.stm|title=Leeds 6–30 Warrington|publisher=BBC|date=28 August 2010|access-date=22 November 2019|first=Ged|last=Scott}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |location=London |work=The Guardian |title=Chris Hicks hat-trick leads Warrington to Challenge Cup triumph over Leeds |first=Andy |last=Wilson |date=28 August 2010 |access-date=22 November 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100831105934/http://www.guardian.co.uk/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |archive-date=31 August 2010 |url-status=live}}</ref>

===Retirement and return to NRL===
On 3 December 2010, it was announced that Hicks had retired with immediate effect to make room for Warrington's new signing, [[Joel Monaghan]]. However, the retirement was very short-lived. Within a fortnight, Hicks signed a one-year deal with the [[Parramatta Eels]] to help fill the gap left by the retiring [[Eric Grothe Jr]]. {{Citation needed|date=March 2016}} Hicks retired at the end the [[2011 NRL season]]. In round 26 against the [[Sydney Roosters]], Hicks scored a try in his 200th Game.
